default.summary: Grid connection timing varies by utility and ISO. Check local transmission operator and queue timelines.

CA.summary: CAISO: High demand for interconnection; queue wait often 5–7+ years. Plan early and expect study cycles.

TX.summary: ERCOT: Large queue; wait times vary by zone (often 3–5 years). CREZ and Panhandle have renewable build-out.

NM.summary: PNM / SPP: Moderate queue; interconnection timelines typically 3–5 years. Growing solar and H2 interest.

AZ.summary: APS, TEP, others: Desert Southwest queue; expect 4–6 years. Strong solar overlap.

NV.summary: NV Energy: Queue and study process; typical timelines 4–6 years for new generation.

WA.summary: BPA / utilities: Pacific Northwest; hydro-dominated. New generation interconnection varies by utility.

OR.summary: PGE, PacifiCorp: Queue timelines typically 4–6 years; high renewable interest.

WY.summary: SPP / PacifiCorp: Wind and transmission build-out; interconnection timelines vary (3–6 years).

UT.summary: PacifiCorp, Dominion: Moderate queue; expect 4–5 years for new projects.

CO.summary: Xcel, SPP, others: Active renewable queue; typical wait 4–6 years.

IA.summary: MISO: Major wind and renewable queue; interconnection often 4–6 years.

OK.summary: SPP: Significant wind and solar queue; timelines typically 3–5 years.

KS.summary: SPP: High renewable interest; queue wait often 4–5 years.

IL.summary: MISO/PJM: Dense transmission; queue timelines 5–7+ years in congested areas.

NY.summary: NYISO: Queue and upgrade requirements; expect 5–7 years for new generation.

PA.summary: PJM: Large interconnection queue; typical wait 5–7 years.

OH.summary: PJM: Congested zones; queue wait often 5–7 years.

FL.summary: FPL, Duke, others: Florida utilities; interconnection timelines vary (4–6 years).

LA.summary: Entergy, SPP: Gulf region; queue and study process typically 4–5 years.
